title: Paper-guided graph-aware pretrain warmup

queries: graph-aware language model pretraining graph prompt tabular llm alignment
reconstructive graph instruction tuning auxiliary pretraining graph token llm

papers.title: Graph-Aware Language Model Pre-Training on a Large Graph Corpus Can Help Multiple Graph Applications
papers.claim: Graph-aware pretraining improves downstream transfer across multiple graph tasks more reliably than local prompt-side tweaks.
papers.relevance: Supports spending the next Stage 3 slot on representation/alignment pretraining rather than another basis coefficient scan.

papers.title: Toward Graph-Tokenizing Large Language Models with Reconstructive Graph Instruction Tuning
papers.claim: Stronger graph-alignment supervision improves graph-token transfer and reduces text-dominant bias.
papers.relevance: Supports testing an explicit warmup phase before task finetuning, even with the currently available pretrain path.

papers.title: Can we Soft Prompt LLMs for Graph Learning Tasks?
papers.claim: Alignment quality is a central bottleneck for graph soft prompts.
papers.relevance: Supports shifting the next candidate from prompt/basis mixing to representation warmup.

evidence.why_now: Prompt-side, basis-side, and simple retrieval-side scans have all failed to produce a non-regressive bundle. The paper shortlist already pointed toward representation/alignment pretraining as the next higher-value slot, and the current codebase has an existing pretrain path that can be screened immediately.

hypothesis: A short graph-aware pretrain warmup before task finetuning can improve cross-task stability by strengthening the graph-text interface upstream of prompt injection. Even the existing masked-cell / statement-correctness pretrain path may provide a better initialization than continuing to hand-tune basis residual coefficients.
notes: This is the first pretraining-side Stage 3 screening candidate under the refreshed 512-step protocol.
Keep the rest of the strict screening setup unchanged; only add a short pretrain warmup.
If this fails broadly, the next step should likely require a more explicit alignment/reconstruction loss change rather than another no-code scan.
Launched on 2026-05-05 under the refreshed 512-step strict baseline.
All three runs failed before the first evaluation step because the current pretrain path expects batch[select_table].df, which is not present on the remote NodeStorage objects.
Judge could not produce a metric report because no evaluation lines were emitted; treat this bundle as implementation-blocked and failed.
